description: Comprehensive single-gene analysis via gget: search -> info -> seq (nucleotide + protein) -> archs4 tissue/correlation -> opentargets diseases/drugs. Writes CSV + FASTA reports under record_store (read-only remote queries).
rules: read-only — only queries remote databases (Ensembl, UniProt, NCBI, ARCHS4, OpenTargets) and writes local files
needs network and an installed gget package; with gget absent the porter degrades gracefully and still writes a non-empty report
all outputs land under record_store (the porter runs the vendored core there)
no remote mutation, no install — gget setup / alphafold are out of scope
usage: Set GENE (gene symbol, e.g. ACE2) and SPECIES (default homo_sapiens). Output: gene_analysis.log (run log) plus gget CSV/FASTA files (<gene>_info.csv, <gene>_nucleotide.fasta, <gene>_protein.fasta, <gene>_tissue_expression.csv, <gene>_correlated_genes.csv, <gene>_diseases.csv, <gene>_drugs.csv).
limitation: Requires gget + network to produce real biological results; offline it only produces the run log. Processes one gene per run. Per-step gget failures are warned and skipped, not fatal.
